name: SBI IRCTC Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Spend ₹1,00,000 or more in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 10% value back on IRCTC train ticket bookings (capped at ₹1,000 per quarter)
1% value back on all other transactions

redemptionOptions: Gift vouchers from SBI Card catalogue

welcomeBenefits.condition: On first IRCTC transaction within 30 days of card issuance

milestoneBenefits.benefit: Annual fee waiver for next year

additionalBenefits: 1% transaction charge waiver on IRCTC bookings
Reward Points can be used directly for train bookings
pros: 10% value back on IRCTC bookings is the highest in the segment for train travel
Transaction charge waiver on IRCTC saves additional money
Low annual fee of ₹500 with ₹1 lakh waiver threshold
Reward Points directly usable for train bookings
Niche card that delivers strong value for regular train travelers
cons: IRCTC cashback capped at ₹1,000 per quarter limits savings for heavy travelers
1% value back on non-IRCTC spends is below average
No lounge access — not ideal for broader travel needs
Limited utility if you don't travel by train frequently
idealFor: Frequent Indian Railways travelers who book regularly through IRCTC
Budget travelers looking to save on train ticket costs
Users who want a niche travel card with low annual fees

faqs.question: How does the 10% value back on IRCTC work?
faqs.answer: When you book train tickets on IRCTC using this card, you get 10% of the transaction value back as Reward Points. This is capped at ₹1,000 per quarter (₹4,000 per year maximum).

faqs.question: Can I use Reward Points for IRCTC bookings?
faqs.answer: Yes, accumulated Reward Points can be redeemed directly for IRCTC train ticket bookings, making the redemption process seamless for regular travelers.

faqs.question: Is the IRCTC transaction fee waived?
faqs.answer: Yes, SBI waives the 1% IRCTC transaction/convenience fee when you use this card for bookings, providing additional savings on top of the reward points.

faqs.question: What is the annual fee waiver condition?
faqs.answer: The ₹500 annual fee is waived if your total annual spends exceed ₹1,00,000 in the previous year.
